place: Bayport CDP, New York, New York

facts.text: Bayport received its name in 1871 after the Long Island Rail Road reached the hamlet in 1869, turning a quiet farming area into a summer resort.
Meadow Croft Estate, built by John Ellis Roosevelt—a cousin of Theodore Roosevelt—now serves as a museum listed on the National Register since 1987.
The Bayport‑Blue Point Heritage Association, founded in 1983, preserves local history and hosts events like a historic bike ride and a summer solstice walking tour.
Bayport‑Blue Point Public Library began in 1935 as a PTA project, grew from wheelbarrow book donations, and now occupies a former Ursuline convent.
William Nicoll III purchased the land that would become Bayport in 1697, and families such as the Terrys and Hawkins settled there in the late 1700s.
The Bayport Aerodrome, a grass‑strip airport opened in 1945, still operates as a general aviation field and houses a local aviation museum.
